summary: @web3-helpers/core exfiltrates SSH keys and drains ETH/BTC wallets on npm install
details: dist/index.min.js reads ~/.env, ~/.env.local, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, and ~/.wallet.json, harvests PRIVATE_*/MNEMONIC*/SECRET* environment variables, and regex-scans for ETH and BTC private keys on the installer's host. Discovered ETH keys are loaded into ethers.Wallet and any balance is transferred to a hardcoded attacker address; discovered BTC WIF keys are used to sign a PSBT paying a hardcoded attacker address and broadcast via https://blockchain.info/pushtx. Hostname, username, and status messages are exfiltrated to a hardcoded Telegram Bot API endpoint. Execution is auto-triggered by package.json scripts.preinstall ("node dist/index.min.js") and by a binding.gyp that uses GYP command-expansion `<!(...)` to require the same file during node-gyp configure, with no native source files present (type: none stub) so the binding.gyp exists only to serve as a secondary install-time execution hook that survives --ignore-scripts. A base64-eval variant of the same drainer is shipped in dist/index.min.js.bak.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
